summary: A symmetrical stable or barn structure with a distinctive domed/rounded brick roof, flanked by two tall pillars with flat caps. The main building features a brick dome roof, white fence railings, and a multi-level interior with horizontal striped walls in cream and tan tones. Two tall towers with dark stone pillars support overhanging caps on either side. The structure sits on a dirt foundation with white block perimeter fencing.

shape.overall_form: Rectangular main body with symmetrical tall pillars on left and right sides, domed roof structure

materials.name: wood
materials.role: horizontal wall stripes and interior framing

rebuild_hints: Build two tall pillars (approximately 2 blocks wide, 10 blocks tall) on left and right sides with dark stone/stonebrick
Create flat stone slab caps on top of each pillar with overhang
Construct main rectangular body between pillars with cream/tan sandstone base layers
Add horizontal wood stripe pattern to walls (alternating wood and sandstone blocks)
Build domed/rounded brick roof using stacked brick blocks in curved pattern
Install white fence railings around entire perimeter and interior divisions
Create interior stall divisions with horizontal fence rails at multiple heights
Ensure bilateral symmetry across the north-south centerline
Add dirt foundation around entire structure with white block perimeter outline
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and number of stalls not fully visible from all angles
Precise height of pillars difficult to determine; estimate based on proportions
Some wall material may be different sandstone variants rather than single type
Roof construction method (solid dome vs. hollow frame) unclear from exterior views
Exact dimensions of interior divisions uncertain
